---
title: "Practicum 2.1"
output: html_document
---

### Hypothesis: I believe we are able to distinguish between defensive and offensive players based on their skill attributes.

library(tidyverse)

players <- readr::read_csv("/Users/lukas/Documents/Skola/Ing./2. semester/OZNAL/Data/players_22.csv")

dim(players)
glimpse(players)

## Result variable: I decided to choose a binary result variable which will evaluate to which class a players falls into: DEFENSIVE or OFFENSIVE

positions <- players %>%
  pull(club_position) %>%
  str_split(",") %>%
  unlist() %>%
  str_trim() %>%
  unique()

positions    

## After listing all possible positions a player can be assigned, we need to filter out ones that don't express their offensive/defensive nature- for example, a goalkeeper is neither offensive or defensive, or midfielders without special assignment could mean both.

offensive_pos <- c("ST", "CF", "LW", "RW", "CAM")
defensive_pos <- c("CB", "LB", "RB", "LWB", "RWB", "CDM")


## Here we distinguish which player positions fall into which class

players_classified <- players %>%
mutate(player_classification = case_when(
  club_position %in% offensive_pos ~ "offensive",
  club_position %in% defensive_pos ~ "defensive",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)) %>%
filter(!is.na(player_classification))

players_classified
count(players_classified, player_classification)


## Here we filter out the dataset, so all ambiguous players are removed

class_table <- players_classified %>%
  count(player_classification)

class_table

ggplot(class_table, aes(x = player_classification, y = n, fill = player_classification)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  labs(
    title = "Player count: offensive vs defensive",
    x = "Class",
    y = "Player count"
  )

## Visualisation of the count of players in offensive and defensive class based on the filtered out dataset

cat(names(players_classified), sep = "\n")

  attribute_overview <- players_classified %>%
    summarise(across(
      everything(),
      list(
        type = ~ class(.x)[1],
        missing = ~ sum(is.na(.x)),
        unique_values = ~ n_distinct(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        example = ~ as.character(first(na.omit(.x)))
      ),
      .names = "{.col}__{.fn}"
    )) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = everything(),
      names_to = c("attribute", ".value"),
      names_sep = "__"
    ) %>%
    arrange(attribute)

  attribute_overview
